name: Sarah Mitchell
venue_or_festival: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
notes: Booking window: 6-12 months out. Submit EPK through official submission portal. Acts need significant streaming presence (1M+ monthly). Headliners confirmed 18 months in advance.

name: James O'Connor
notes: UK's most prestigious festival. Application via official form only. Strong UK profile required. Emerging stages for developing acts — Avalon, BBC Introducing.

name: Priya Kapoor
notes: Multi-day festival with multiple stages. Perry's stage for electronic acts. Preferred booking agent contact required for main stage. International extensions: Brazil, Chile, Argentina, France, Germany, Sweden.

name: Carlos Espinoza
notes: Applications open in August for March festival. Official showcase pays $100-250. Unofficial showcases pay nothing but offer visibility. Must have US work authorization.

name: Emma Thornton
notes: Requires major label or large independent booking agency. Acts must have demonstrated sell-out history in smaller markets first. Minimum 6-month advance booking.

name: Daniel Kim
notes: Tennessee camping festival with strong community ethos. Emerging stage for developing acts. Daytime sets for new artists. Strong live performance reputation essential.

name: Nina Rossi
notes: Barcelona-based tastemaker festival. Critical acclaim matters more than streaming numbers. Strong Pitchfork/NME/The FADER press history helps. Extensions in Madrid, Porto, Buenos Aires.

name: Marcus Johnson
notes: Historic Black music venue. Strong connection to Black music legacy preferred. Amateur Night still open to undiscovered artists. Main stage requires booking agent.

name: Yuki Yamamoto
notes: Japan's premier international music festival. Japanese booking agent connection helpful. 12-month booking lead time. Strong live performance required.

name: Amara Diallo
notes: Black culture festival with strong community values. Artist must align with AFROPUNK ethos. Extensions in Atlanta, Paris, London, Johannesburg. No corporate sellout acts.

name: Jake Morrison
notes: Historic venue, strong touring circuit stop. LiveNation/AEG operated. Local touring history in Bay Area helps. 3-6 month advance booking.

name: Claire Beaumont
notes: Record store venue with discerning indie audience. In-store performances and ticketed shows. Pitchfork-adjacent audience. Often tied to vinyl release events.

name: Rafael Torres
notes: Mid-size LA venue. LA local touring history helps. Strong Latin music programming. 2-4 month advance booking. Willing to work with developing acts.

name: Olivia Patel
notes: North London venue on established UK touring circuit. Strong student and indie audience. 3-month booking lead time. Accepts developing international acts with strong UK press.

name: Sam Washington
notes: DC institution. Consistently voted best club in America. Booking through agent preferred but accepts strong independent pitches with regional draw.

name: Lena Kovacs
notes: World-famous techno club. Electronic and techno only. Resident DJ connections help. Guest DJ bookings made 3-6 months out. No mainstream acts.

name: Tony Graves
notes: Legendary West Hollywood venue. Intimate setting for developing acts. Acoustic-friendly shows. Strong songwriter tradition. Local LA scene connection helps.

name: Hana Suzuki
notes: Key Tokyo venue for international touring acts. Japanese promoter relationship helpful. Marketing support for international acts. 6-month booking lead time for Japan tours.

name: Derek Hamilton
notes: Historic Nashville venue. Country and Americana roots strongly represented. Strong touring history in Southeast required. Booking agent preferred.

name: Adaeze Obi
notes: Major UK touring stop. AEG/O2 Academy circuit. Strong UK streaming and press required. 4-6 month booking lead time.

name: Maria Santos
notes: Brooklyn's go-to for emerging acts. Ground floor bookings available. Local NYC following helps but not required.

name: Finn MacRae
notes: Small Bristol venue. Great for UK tours. Local press in Southwest England helps. Open to self-booked emerging acts.

name: Isabella Cruz
notes: Miami-based Latin music promoter. Organizes club nights and small venue shows. Growing fanbase in South Florida essential.

name: Marcus Webb
notes: Philly alternative venue. DIY friendly. No guarantee for emerging acts — door deal common. Philly regional following valued.

name: Talia Greenwood
notes: Midlands indie record store venue. UK Midlands touring circuit stop. Free in-store events plus paid evening shows.

name: Pascal Renaud
notes: Paris indie venue. French-language pitches preferred. European tour dates valued. Good stepping stone to French market.

name: Kenji Mori
notes: Intimate Tokyo indie venue. Shimokitazawa music neighborhood. Great for debut Japan shows. Japanese label or promoter connection helps.

name: Rosa Martinez
notes: Intimate Barcelona experimental venue. Spanish-language pitch appreciated. Open to unknown international artists. Good starting point for Spain.

name: Jerome Williams
notes: Cleveland Midwest touring circuit. Good for coast-to-coast tours. Midwest regional following a plus. Willing to develop new acts.

name: Aaliya Mensah
notes: UK-based African music promoter. Organizes club nights and showcase events. Great entry point for African artists breaking into UK market.
